items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Abby, we've arrived. How do you feel now?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Abby?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Nothing from my Tacet Mark. Is it sleeping? Or is it...)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Cartethyia helped with Abby's symptoms before... I could really use her help now.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (We share the same thread of Imperator's power. I could try using that to sense where she is.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (What's that creature she's facing? A half-human, half-lion Tacet Discord? Or a special Echo?)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (She was here before.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (I'll follow this same path and see if I can find someone to talk to.)

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: As your fingers touch the cold statue, the whispers of fallen heroes ring through your mind.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: The clash of weapons mingles with battle cries and hearty laughter. Amid these sounds, the <te href=850166>Hero's Legacy Armor</te> murmurs its late master's eternal longing for glory...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (That armor might be an Echo.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (An Echo... that awaits a true warrior.)
Alright, Emilio. Why don't you take your band of cowards and get out of my way before I decide to handle it myself?
Pulling dirty tricks before the Agon... did you think I wouldn't notice? Did you ever stop to think you nearly got an innocent person killed? Shame on you!

items.speaker: Emilio
items.text: Lupa… so you're really not gonna reconsider? Even with the finals just around the corner?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Reconsider? Ha! You think I should keep playing along and stay blind to your match-fixing?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I don't care what you do off the field, but in the arena, fairness isn't negotiable. If you can't get that, just keep wagging your tail for the house...

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: You know, I once thought you were a decent Gladiator, and we fought well together. But look at you now. Pathetic.

items.speaker: Emilio
items.text: You—! You know nothing, Lupa.

items.speaker: Emilio
items.text: ...Not everyone has the same strength to survive in Septimont with a clean conscience.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Sorry about that clown show. Don't judge Septimont by a few rats that can't hold their own in the dirt. They're not the norm here.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Lupa's the name. <te href=850164>Gladiator</te> by trade. Guess we're acquainted now after crossing blades. Care to tell me your name?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Got it. It's rare to meet someone with your kind of strength.

options.text: I take down Tacets Discords every day.
Thanks. Your moves are impressive too.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Combat makes the greatest warriors. You're a fine example of that.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Ah, so you like hunting Tacet Discords? Then you've come to the right place.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I'm used to fighting the Gladiators here, but the way you handled your blade... it's beautiful. I like it.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: What happened just now? Did they try to ambush you?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Heh... just about. That man, Emilio, used to be my partner. <te href=850169>The Silvas</te> were probably behind this.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The House of Silva is nobility in Septimont. I share their name, though, I'm sure they've thrown me out since I stepped away from the team.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Is this tied to the match-fixing you mentioned?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Yes. I knew something was off, but I didn't think they'd go this far. Maybe they threw some serious money at it.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I thought the money I made them would've been enough. Oh well!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I was thinking, you're not a local, are you? How about—

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: We team up and fight together in the finals?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (That's Cartethyia's frequency... but how?)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Huh? {PlayerName}, is that your way of saying yes?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I'm sorry. Lupa, have you ever met a Ragunnesi girl by the name of Cartethyia?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Cartethyia... No, never heard of her. What's going on with her?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: She's missing. I'm looking for her.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I tracked her to Septimont, and… it seems she confronted Arsinosa.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Arsinosa is the symbol of our spirit, the great Echo guarding Septimont for centuries. Besides the current <te href=850157>Ephor</te>, Augusta, nobody has ever faced her directly.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I don't know if this Cartethyia of yours is strong enough to take on Arsinosa. If she did, it would be the talk of Septimont.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I see… no leads yet, then.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Well, that's why you should join me in the Agon. When Arsinosa shows up in the finals, you might find the answers you're looking for.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The champion gets noble recognition, a hefty bonus, and some secret perks. If we win, you can take it all.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: If that doesn't interest you, then the attention that comes with being a champion will help you find the information you need on her.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: True. But what's in it for you?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Me? I just want to see the look on their cheating faces when they realize I've beaten them at their own game!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: They're probably betting against me in secret. Ha, doesn't matter. Once we win, they'll lose it all. Serves 'em right.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: If there's no punishment for crossing the line, then what's the point of a line at all?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I don't know what other tricks my dear family has planned, but you? You're an outlander. Strong, too. No ties to their schemes.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: That's why I look forward to walking this path with you. Can't wait to see what kind of Gladiator you'll be!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Arsinosa and the unusual frequency I felt from Lupa are the only leads I've got…)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Lupa denies ever meeting Cartethyia, but maybe she didn't realize... or she's hiding something.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Either way, teaming up with her is my best shot at finding the truth.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: That's the spirit! Well then...  I'll be counting on you, my fellow warrior.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Now, let's go register our <te href=850155>team</te>!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: We did it!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: That was perfection! Haven't felt this alive in ages.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I could finally focus and savor every moment of the fight…

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Thanks, warrior. Teaming up with you was the right choice.

options.text: You flatter me. I enjoyed it just as much.
Fighting alongside Echoes was an entirely new thrill.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: And these are...?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Ta-das. These little ones love a good match and never miss a chance to cheer for the victors.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: They're more fired up than usual. Looks like your performance really won them over, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: They're just as lively as the baby Gryphon I came across before.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: This city does know how to keep spirits high! Speaking of Gryphons, they've actually been here long before our ancestors even settled on this archipelago.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Anyway, we've secured our first victory. The next match will be held further out.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: If we take down the top-ranked team there, we'll be… Huh?

items.speaker: Eager Spectator
items.text: CONTENDER LUPA! PLEASE SIGN FOR ME!

items.speaker: Flustered Spectator
items.text: I-it's you… in the flesh! Why-why would a star like you be in this arena?

items.speaker: Flustered Spectator
items.text: It's impossible to get a ticket for your matches... Ugh, I wish I'd known sooner that you'd be here!

items.speaker: Discerning Spectator
items.text: Did something happen with the Bough of Legions, Contender Lupa? Who's this new Gladiator you've got with you? Did you replace Contender Emilio?

items.speaker: Discerning Spectator
items.text: Oh, that's front-page material...

items.speaker: Eager Spectator
items.text: Hey, make some room! LUPA! I LOVED YOUR PERFORMANCE! PLEASE, WILL YOU SIGN FOR ME?!！

options.text: You're quite a celebrity around here, Lupa!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I'm sorry. It's been too long since I've fought outside the Colosseum. Should've worn a helmet.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: It comes with the job. Gotta draw a crowd in, both in and out of the arena.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Don't worry! I'm confident we'll claim the title. Soon enough, you'll be signing autographs 'til your hand bleeds.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Alright, everyone…

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Uh... I-I can handle this, but it'll take too long.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: We need to make a swift retreat.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Okay, but how?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Easy. Just hold my hand and don't let go!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: So… this is Septimont.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The traces will guide you here, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: And, when the time comes, you'll bring this story to its rightful end.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The scent of seawater... It's right ahead.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: No response.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Can you tell where these traces lead, Abby?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Over there. But the traces are kinda messy. They feel unsteady too...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: That way… That's the archipelago across from Ragunna.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The traces are still fresh. We should follow them to find Cartethyia before they fade.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Should we go now?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Yes. With the Threnodian's will gone, she wouldn't lose contact unless something's wrong. Really wrong.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: But before we go…

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Oh, a call from the honored Laureate {Male=himself;Female=herself}. What is it? Missing the serenity of my castle, I presume?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Cartethyia is missing… I see.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: It is indeed a rather urgent issue.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Go on, what is it you wish to ask of me?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I want to learn more about the archipelago before I set off to find her.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Ah, caution is indeed a virtue. Since you're in a hurry, I'll spare you the lengthy details.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: That archipelago is called Septimont.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: It's another city-state within Rinascita. Like us Ragunnesi, its people were guided by the Sentinel across vast seas to settle there.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: But over time, Septimont and Ragunna's differences led to a rift. Each one forged its own path.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: What kind of differences?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Septimontians don't believe in the Sentinel.

options.text: I thought it was a common faith across Rinascita.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Even from a shared origin, the hearts and minds of people can diverge in unexpected ways.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Septimont is a city of warriors. Its creed is simple "All for glory, fear no death." They revere heroes, not the Divinity.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Under The Order's control, Ragunna enforces strict border controls for Septimontians, but Septimont places no such barriers for the Ragunnesi.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: You could say that Septimont has a far more accepting attitude toward outlanders.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: At present, Septimont is hosting the Great Agon, a cornerstone in the city's economy. Such a season draws visitors from far and wide.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Sounds like the perfect time to visit Septimont. Thank you, Cantarella.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: You're the Fisalias' eternal ally... and of course, my esteemed guest. No need for formalities between us.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Its frequency is similar to the Echoes in Ragunna. Could it have originated from Imperator's power as well?)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (But it's unstable, as if something else is interfering... Strange, it's just like Abby said.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (I can feel its source close to the city, but can't pin down where exactly.)
Boss, you're looking so serious!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: And you are...?

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Me? I'm a traveling Taoist from <te href=850154>Mengzhou</te>. Just call me Buling.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Exorcism, talisman divination, fortune-telling... you name it, I "bling" it! So {PlayerName}, you want to try your luck fah?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: How did you know my name?

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Heard about you from an old friend. You know Jianxin? Pretty sure you met her in Jinzhou. She trained with the same master before me.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: We've got history. Last time I went up to the mountain to see her, she mentioned you.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: So I hear you're looking for someone? Tsk, not so easy, not so easy. The feng shui in Rinascita is not good.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: The Qi is trapped underwater, not good flow. No luck now, bad sign. You should be careful, Boss!

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: You show up and the stars change... I was betting on who could break the deadlock, so I'm glad I caught you first.
Sniff... Hm?
Not bad!
Think you can keep me from fighting?
Careful now...
We'll see who rips whose throat out first.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Cartethyia... standing on the water?)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (That abnormal sensation came over me again when my sword struck the contender's Echo. )

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (All the Echoes in Septimont seem to exhibit unstable power. Something is definitely off.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (But it seems like no one else has noticed.)

items.speaker: Whispering Spectator
items.text: Contender Lupa isn't in the Bough of Legions anymore... what a shame.

items.speaker: Doubtful Spectator
items.text: Just two days before the finals, as well! It's unfair to her former teammates and her fans.

items.speaker: Whispering Spectator
items.text: Is it about the money? She's changed teammates how many times now? As for that newcomer next to her...

items.speaker: Scornful Specatator
items.text: Without the Silvas' patronage, there's no way a gladiator from the outskirts could rise to fame so quickly.

items.speaker: Disgruntled Spectator
items.text: Quiet down. Where are you hearing these rumors? Open your eyes, Lupa has never once thrown a match!

items.speaker: Doubtful Spectator
items.text: Hmph, everyone thinks they know the real truth. But consider this, it's a fact I know to be true...

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Let's go, my fellow warrior.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: It's all going as planned! Let's get you settled somewhere comfortable, then tomorrow, we head to the Colosseum Olymdos for the semi-finals.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I've been meaning to ask, is there a body of water in the city? I think Cartethyia might've passed through there.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: A body of water? Yes, but it's been abandoned for years. That elevator can take you to the upper city, though.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: If you want to try looking for her, we can start there!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Thanks. And…

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Yes?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Those people just now… they talked about match-fixing. Don't you want to challenge that?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I didn't have time to gather enough proof before I left. Besides, this isn't the right moment.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Once we win the Great Agon, I'll make everything public. In Septimont, only the winners get to speak their truth.

options.text: Their slander gets under my skin.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Hehe, I'm fine. High praise leads to harsh words. It's always like that.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Heh, a strong warrior with a kind heart... Rare traits for a hunter.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Honestly, I don't care what people say. This kind of thing happens every day in Septimont. If you can't handle it, you won't last.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Tarnishing rivals' reputations is one of their usual tricks. I cut off their betting profits, so it's only natural they'd be upset.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Besides, now I have a reliable warrior by my side. That's all that matters.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: No worrying about whether my teammate can keep up, no dealing with petty politics... just the thrill of the fight.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Thank you for that, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: This is the place. I'll look for Cartethyia's traces here.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: How?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Through... the connection we share.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Alright then. I'll wait here. Good luck!

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: You call upon the power of Imperator, calling for the one who shares its essence.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: The unique frequency pulses through your blade and spreads across the water in ripples. But the connection weakens, fading into the distance.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Cartethyia isn't here.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: How's it going? You look... frustrated.

options.text: I'm okay. We still have the lead on Arsinosa.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Don't worry, we can follow the lead on Arsinosa.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: That's right! I'm sure you'll make progress by the finals.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Tercules… His statues once stood tall on both sides of the street, watching over the heroes of Septimont as they set out to hunt.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Back then, the chimes drifting up from the city were the finest sounds of glory.

items.speaker: Excited Villager
items.text: Ha, I knew it! The Blue Furies remain undefeated!

items.speaker: Excited Villager
items.text: Ah, folk always write 'em off, thinking they're just sailors or singers. That don't matter in the arena. It's all about pure strength.

items.speaker: Cautious Villager
items.text: Most of the teams in this year's finals aren't even backed by local nobles.

items.speaker: Excited Villager
items.text: So what? Worried about your bets? How many times do I have to—

items.speaker: Cautious Villager
items.text: Aye.

items.speaker: Excited Villager
items.text: Alright, alright... Oh, look here, a visitor! Welcome to Septimont!

items.speaker: Excited Villager
items.text: Your clothes aren't from around here. You from the New Federation?

options.text: Actually, I'm from the Black Shores.
From the endless void I come...

items.speaker: Excited Villager
items.text: Ah, I've met some visitors from Huanglong before. Proper folk, polite as can be. They do tend to speak in riddles though!

items.speaker: Excited Villager
items.text: Hmm... sounds familiar, but I can't say I know where that is.

items.speaker: Excited Villager
items.text: Makin' jokes already? I like your style! Anyway, enjoy your stay here, friend!

options.text: What were you talking about just now?
About the finals you were discussing…

items.speaker: Excited Villager
items.text: We just watched a live match. Too bad you missed it. Mattia from the Blue Furies gave the other team a right pounding!

items.speaker: Cautious Villager
items.text: Aye. <te href=850167>Capitoline Hill</te>'s holding the <te href=850149>Great Agon</te> now in the city. Happens every four years. All of Septimont's talkin' about who'll be the next champion. You here for the games too?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: No, I'm looking for someone.

items.speaker: Excited Villager
items.text: At this time? That might be tricky. Folks from all over Solaris are floodin' into Septimont lately!

items.speaker: Cautious Villager
items.text: Who's it you're looking for? This here is the first stop for ferries heading to Capitoline Hill. Maybe we've seen them before.

items.speaker: Cautious Villager
items.text: ...Can't say I've seen anyone like that around.

items.speaker: Cautious Villager
items.text: Most Ragunnesi we see here are from the Troupe of Fools or the Montelli nobles. They're a bit more open to our ways of thinking about faith.

items.speaker: Cautious Villager
items.text: If she's wearing the Order's emblem, though, she'll have drawn some eyes.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (After Cartethyia and Fleurdelys's frequencies merged, she got her body back. If she passed through here, people would've seen her.)

items.speaker: Excited Villager
items.text: A half-human, half-lion creature wearin' armor and swingin' a poleaxe? That's gotta be the <te href=850168>Guardian Echo</te> of Septimont, our Lioness of Glory, Arsinosa!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Can you tell me more about this Guardian Echo?

items.speaker: Cautious Villager
items.text: Gladly! See, ever since our ancestors set foot on this land, we Septimontians have fought the harsh elements and the savage Tacet Discords.

items.speaker: Cautious Villager
items.text: Many brave souls came before us, their Reverberations settlin' in the stone that now stands as <ano=Lioness of Glory>Arsinosa</ano>. She was born from the battle's roar and the fierce bellows of pride.

items.speaker: Excited Villager
items.text: As the guardian of Septimont, Arsinosa usually only reveals herself during the Great Agon. To earn her blessing is the highest honor any Septimontian could hope for!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (But... why would Cartethyia take on Septimont's Guardian Echo?)

items.speaker: Excited Villager
items.text: If you want to see her, just wait for the finals. They'll be held in the Colosseum in the city. But don't dawdle, get a ticket now or you'll miss out!

items.speaker: Cautious Villager
items.text: As for that Order girl you're looking for, you might try askin' in the inns around the city...

items.speaker: Busy Septimotian
items.text: Clay! Edwin! Come give me a hand!

items.speaker: Cautious Villager
items.text: Ah, right, we best be off. If you want to see the fights, follow this path to the arena. The ticket booth's just up ahead.

items.speaker: Valius
items.text: {Cus:Var,VarType=Global Key=main_team_name} versus Blue Furies.

items.speaker: Valius
items.text: Let the Agon begin!

items.speaker: Hermilia
items.text: {Cus:Var,VarType=Global Key=main_team_name} versus Vulcan Mining.

items.speaker: Hermilia
items.text: Let the Agon begin!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: What was that smirk about?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Oh, me? I just... sent word to certain people.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Too much modesty is a double-edged sword, so I'll be frank. When it comes to bankability, I'm at the top of the game.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: That's how my family, the Silvas, have kept their position all these years. But the nobles here in Septimont are always hunting and contesting for more.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Lions and wolves tear each other to pieces, while the hyenas lie in wait, eyes on their bellies. Everyone wants a taste of the juiciest meat.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: And with the illegal betting they're running... Once they hear I've left the old team, they'll all be scrambling to get a piece of the action.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Are you planning to muddy the waters?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The waters are already so murky no one can tell who's stirring them.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: But it's still better now than it was before. The mess they made before the current Ephor took over was...
May applause greet you, Lupa.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Ha... I see you've been waiting for me, Cartur.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: He was my manager back in the Bough of Legions. Has a gift for knowing exactly what to say to anyone, though that's his flaw, too.

items.speaker: Cartur
items.text: For a manager, isn't the skill of speech always a gift?

items.speaker: Cartur
items.text: Domina Julia Silva hoped you'd behave and return home. She bid that I reason with you at the earliest opportunity.

items.speaker: Cartur
items.text: But... it seems you've found yourself a new ally. I daresay her hopes are dashed.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: And? What's your point?

items.speaker: Cartur
items.text: How disappointing. Not even a hint of charm when there's no crowd around to impress.

items.speaker: Cartur
items.text: Feeling confident about this new team of yours? What do you think your chances are of winning the championship?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: With me and {PlayerName}? One hundred percent.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Enough, Cartur. Spare me the games and empty chatter! I have one question for you...

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: ...Are you involved in the Silvas' match-fixing?

items.speaker: Cartur
items.text: No. I do love money, but I don't stoop to that level to get it.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Fair enough. You may leave now.

items.speaker: Cartur
items.text: Tut, tut, dangerous question, Lupa. Think carefully. The Silvas may have made you a star, but they can just as easily snuff that star out.

items.speaker: Cartur
items.text: I've never doubted your talent, but these are the rules of the game.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I know the rules well enough.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: ...I just wonder if Domina Julia truly understands who's making the rules now, and where she stands in this game.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: (Someone's still alive.)

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Are you okay? You seem like a local… are there any other survivors?

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: I'm managing. I-I can still move, but I'm exhausted. I've walked for so long, for so far... but I still can't leave this city.

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: The white <te href=850161>Tidal Blight</te> follows me wherever I go.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: (Tidal Blight… could she mean the Dark Tide?)

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: What's your name?

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: I… don't remember.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: That's alright. Memories can be recovered, and so can your name. I've been through something similar myself, and someone helped me find my way back.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I've only just arrived here. All of Septimont is cloaked in mist. Would you mind sharing with me what you still remember?

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: Who… are you?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: My name is Cartethyia. I'm Ragunna's…

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I'm a wandering knight from Ragunna.

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: A wandering knight… ah.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Don't worry, I'll take care of them.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Come forth, and face my blade.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Welcome to my city, Gryphon's Fortress of Capitoline Hill!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Who are they...?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Oh, some minor noble houses that didn't make it into the Agon.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: But in Septimont, strength and power go hand in hand. Nobles get more, but they better prove they're worth it and pull their weight for the city.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The Agon isn't just about fortune. It's a way to find the capable. For nobles, the ability to command and control Echoes is a basic responsibility.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: If the heir of a noble house isn't strong enough, it signals that the house is unfit to hunt down Tacet Discords or protect Septimont.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: So... if a noble's been living it up, they'll end up broke and thrown out of the city after paying high patronage fees for a gladiator who doesn't even make the cut.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: "Strength is everything." That's how it works. You've fought your battles too, or you wouldn't be here.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Might not work like this everywhere, but here in Septimont, with the Dark Tide breathing down our necks, it's our survival code.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: For most Septimontians, the Agon isn't just an event. It's the training ground nobles and commoners alike have to fight in.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The victors show no mercy to the defeated. People here despise weakness, worship strength, and crave glory.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Where you come from don't matter. Prove yourself, and the crowd will scream your name. The spotlight's yours, the arena's your stage.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: But if you can't can't keep up, if you're not chosen... you'll be left in the shadows.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Hi there. Is team registration still open?

items.speaker: Boethia
items.text: May applause greet you. Yes, you can register... Oh wait, you're Contender Lupa!

items.speaker: Boethia
items.text: Do you... wish to register a new team? But aren't you...

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Plans changed. I'm no longer with the Bough of Legions. This is my new partner, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Boethia
items.text: Ah... I see. Allow me to register you again. Have you settled on a name for your new team?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Hmm… {PlayerName}, why don't you do the honors?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: We Septimontians live alongside the waves, and I've always found their sound soothing. A perfect choice!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Haha, I take it you are referring to the two of us. The star does suit you well.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Haha, simple but powerful! A good omen for us, too.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Alright, the name is settled. We'd also like to apply for the special qualifier, please.

items.speaker: Boethia
items.text: Of course. Please wait one moment.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The <te href=850165>Gladiator's Agon</te> happens year-round in Septimont. Training matches, exhibition games, open tournaments, you name it.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: But we're after the Agon of Champions, the biggest event held every four years. We also call it the Great Agon.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: There are two stages: a points-based prelim and an elimination round. The prelim is almost over. The eliminations are about to begin.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I've earned enough points from the regular matches to qualify for the finals, but since you're a new contender, you'll need to win the special qualifier to make it.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: If we defeat higher-ranked opponents, we can secure a spot with fewer matches.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Just the two of us? Is it two-on-two?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Exactly. It's one of the reforms the new Ephor made to the Agon. And of course, our Echo partners will be joining us in the arena too!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Echoes aren't just companions to us, but proof of our strength. Hunting them, taming them, and learning to live with them is the first lesson every Septimontian learns.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Now, it's time to step into the arena. Ready?

items.speaker: Boethia
items.text: Registration completed. Happy hunting, contenders!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Let's see how well we work together in the arena, my fellow warrior.
